title: salix × sobrina
intro: Salix × sobrina, also known as the weeping willow hybrid, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is a cross between the white willow (Salix alba) and the goat willow (Salix caprea). The plant's bark and leaves contain salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making it a valuable medicinal plant. The health benefits of Salix × sobrina include reducing fever, alleviating headaches and arthritis, and acting as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
